pr.title: lb: implement lb_80019628; improve four lbbgflash functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Implemented a C body for lb_80019628 in lb_0192.c and made focused source-shape changes in lbbgflash.c to improve fuzzy matching for several library/background-flash functions. The PR explicitly reported no 100% matches, a green configure+ninja build, and unchanged sibling functions in both touched TUs.

key_files.role: Adds C implementation of lb_80019628 for pad sampling rate/alarm period maintenance.
Adjusts source forms for per-channel saturation, vector squared-component ordering, quaternion rotation pointer handling, and reportedly parent ternary control flow.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: lbbgflash.c summaries for fn_8001FC08, lbBgFlash_80020E38, fn_8002113C, fn_80020AEC
raw_diff.diff: hunks for fn_8001FC08, lbBgFlash_80020E38, fn_8002113C

what_changed.change: Added lb_80019628 as C code. The function snapshots lb_804329F0.x38, updates x0[0].x0, resets x0[0].x8 when needed, chooses a period bounded by OS_TIMER_CLOCK, x0[0].x0, x0[1].x0, and an approximately 1/60-second fps_period, caps the PAD sampling rate at 11 ms, conditionally calls PADSetSamplingRate, and recreates/reschedules the periodic alarm using fn_800195FC.
Reworked four repeated RGBA saturation blocks to introduce branch-local u8 target and f32* cur locals before updating data->x10[i] toward data->x8 channel targets.
Reordered squared component local declarations from dz2/dx2/dy2 to dx2/dy2/dz2 and changed the zero-vector sum order from dz2 + dy2 + dx2 to dx2 + dy2 + dz2.
Introduced Quaternion* volatile rot_ptr = &rot in the non-quaternion path and passed rot_ptr through get/matrix/quat/set calls instead of directly using &rot.
PR body says parent = jobj ? jobj->parent : NULL was expanded to explicit if/else to match original branch scheduling, but the loaded diff excerpt does not include this hunk.

smart_moves.text: The PR body explicitly framed all changes as fuzzy-score improvements rather than claiming full matches, which avoids overstating validation.
For fn_8001FC08, the branch-local target and cur locals are narrow and tied to the stated MWCC register-retention goal rather than broad source churn.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: MWCC keeps byte target and x10[i] pointer in r5/r4

smart_moves.text: For fn_8002113C, the volatile pointer tactic is documented with a specific compiler effect: preserving inline-substituted jobj.h assert calls that direct &rot allowed MWCC to prove away.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Quaternion* volatile local keeps jobj.h:699/700 asserts

smart_moves.text: The PR recorded build/regression scope as python configure.py && ninja with build/GALE01/main.dol OK and sibling functions unchanged.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a repeated per-channel update needs specific MWCC register allocation, branch-local typed temporaries for the byte target and current-value pointer can improve matching without changing the high-level operation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: u8 target and f32* cur introduced in each branch

decomp_lessons.lesson: Local declaration and expression order for squared vector components can affect MWCC floating-point scheduling; try the original-looking dx/dy/dz order when matching lfs/fmuls/fadds pipelines.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: dx2/dy2/dz2 declaration and sum order

decomp_lessons.lesson: A volatile pointer to an otherwise obvious local address can prevent MWCC from proving away null-related checks in an inline-expanded helper, but this is a narrow matching tactic that needs explicit objdiff/build evidence.

Replacing a ternary with explicit if/else may be useful when the target assembly schedules the non-null branch first, but this PR slice only supports that claim from the PR body, not the loaded diff hunk.

naming_conventions.convention: Used short local role names for matching-oriented temporaries: target for the channel byte, cur for the current f32 channel pointer, period/fps_period for timer intervals, rate for PAD sampling rate, and rot_ptr for a pointer to rot.
Kept address-style and existing symbol names such as lb_80019628, fn_8001FC08, lb_804329F0, and x0/x4/x38/x40/x48 rather than inventing semantic global or field names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: added and edited code uses existing address-style/offset-style names

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce branch-local u8 target and f32* cur locals in each saturation branch to keep the byte target and x10[i] pointer in desired registers across rising/falling logic.
Change floating-point declaration and addition order to dx2/dy2/dz2 and dx2 + dy2 + dz2 to better match original lfs/fmuls/fadds scheduling.
Route &rot through Quaternion* volatile rot_ptr so MWCC cannot elide inline-substituted assert calls in the Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation path.
Use explicit if/else instead of a ternary for parent selection when branch order matters.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Loaded diff does not show this hunk, so keep as lower-confidence PR-body evidence.

No human review comments or review records were present in the loaded PR slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Branch-local typed temporaries can steer MWCC register retention in repeated channel blocks
accepted_candidate_records.text: In fn_8001FC08, each RGBA saturation branch was rewritten to use branch-local u8 target and f32* cur locals. The PR body says this kept the byte target and x10[i] pointer in r5/r4 across rising/falling logic and improved fuzzy score from about 89.6% to 91.77%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Floating-point local and sum order can affect vector math scheduling
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lbBgFlash_80020E38, reordering dz2/dx2/dy2 to dx2/dy2/dz2 and using dx2 + dy2 + dz2 was tied to matching lfs/fmuls/fadds scheduling and produced a small fuzzy-score improvement.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Volatile pointer locals can preserve inline-expanded assert paths but require narrow evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: In fn_8002113C, routing &rot through Quaternion* volatile rot_ptr kept MWCC from proving the pointer non-null and eliding Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation inline-substituted asserts at jobj.h:699/700, improving fuzzy score. This should remain a narrow matching tactic, not a default style.

source_update_candidates.title: Document volatile pointer locals as a rare assert-preservation matching tactic
source_update_candidates.text: A PR-specific example used Quaternion* volatile rot_ptr = &rot to prevent MWCC from eliding inline-substituted jobj.h asserts in Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation. Consider adding this as an example under matching tactics only if accompanied by narrow objdiff/build evidence, because volatile locals are already high-risk steering tactics.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already warn that volatile locals need evidence; this PR provides a concrete historical example involving inline assert preservation.

source_update_candidates.title: Add floating-point declaration/order scheduling example
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that for MWCC vector math, local declaration order and expression sum order can affect lfs/fmuls/fadds scheduling, as shown by lbBgFlash_80020E38 changing dz2/dx2/dy2 to dx2/dy2/dz2 and dz2 + dy2 + dx2 to dx2 + dy2 + dz2.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a reusable matching observation, but it should be curated as an example rather than promoted broadly without more cases.

rejection_notes: Do not record PR 2508 as containing 100% matched functions; the PR body explicitly says no 100% matches.
Do not infer human reviewer feedback or rationale; loaded slice has zero review comments and zero reviews.

Do not promote the fn_80020AEC if/else tactic as strongly evidenced from this slice; it appears in the PR body, but not in the loaded raw diff, and the bot report does not list fn_80020AEC as an improved item.
Do not treat the lbbgflash .sdata bot improvement as intentional data work; no diff evidence or PR body explanation for a data-section edit was loaded.
